course_title: Creating classrooms of the future with digital technologies across learning areas

learning_outcomes.description: Review and reflect on the common enablers and barriers to technology integration in your educational context.
Express and evaluate your beliefs about technology, teaching and learning and their influence on your own use of technology.

Compare, evaluate and apply various pedagogical and learning theories in relation to the use of educational technologies and consequential pedagogical affordances for current and emerging technologies in relation to your own pedagogical context.
Critically analyse and debate contested issues associated with existing and emergent technologies with reference to scholarly research and theories.

Design and justify a range of your own technology-enhanced learning activities with reference to your beliefs, learning theories and pedagogies, attributes of effective technology practices and potential barriers to implementation.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Task 1: Concept Map and Reflective Essay
assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will create a concept map that represents your current beliefs about teaching and learning, including your philosophy and pedagogical beliefs. You may use CMap, Inspiration, or another concept/mind mapping software with which you are familiar.

You will use your concept map as the basis for a reflective essay. Your essay should:

  • explain your concept map and the linkages between concepts, giving the reader a clear sense of your teaching and learning philosophy and pedagogical beliefs
  • evaluate how technology can support aspects of your current teaching and learning philosophy.
  • synthesise your current thinking about the role, value, and use of technology in your teaching and learning practice
  • describe the enablers and barriers you perceive for technology integration in your own teaching context, including any concerns and potential opportunities you identify

Your reflection should also demonstrate engagement with the set readings for Module 1 and draw on these readings where appropriate.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submitted electronically by the due date/time via TurnItIn on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Task 2: Lesson Sequence Plan and Interactive Oral
assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will create a four-lesson learning sequence to be delivered through an online learning platform such as Blackboard, Blendspace, Padlet, or another appropriate digital learning environment. The purpose of this task is to demonstrate your understanding of key pedagogical concepts and your ability to make informed decisions about digital learning design for a specific group of learners.

You are not required to create the full lessons for this task. Instead, you will complete the provided lesson sequence template and use it as the basis for an interactive oral.

This assessment has two components:

1. Written lesson sequence plan: approximately 1000 words, plus brief explanatory annotations using the comments feature in Word

You will submit a completed lesson sequence template before your interactive oral. The template should provide a concise but clear overview of your four-lesson learning sequence, including the intended learner group, learning context, syllabus links, learning intentions, success criteria, lesson activities, and key digital tools.

You should use brief annotations, using the comments feature in Word, to justify key design decisions in your plan, such as your selection of learning activities, digital tools, pedagogical strategies, and supports for your intended learner group. There is no set number of annotations required. Students should use annotations where appropriate. Annotations should be concise and should not be used to add a separate essay or extended report.

Your annotations should show how your planning of sequenced learning experiences has considered:

  • students’ diverse knowledge, experiences, characteristics, and cultures
  • understandings of how students learn
  • the pedagogical and technological choices made in the lesson sequence
  • learner diversity, accessibility, engagement, and potential barriers and enablers
  • relevant course readings and/or peer-reviewed literature, where appropriate

Please take into account the particular audience you are targeting, such as a specific age group, culturally diverse students, neurodivergent learners, gifted learners, or learners with special educational needs. Your plan should clearly state the age group for which the learning sequence is intended and consider different ways of learning.

2. Interactive Oral session

You will participate in a 10-minute interactive oral with the lecturer or tutor. This can be completed either in person or via video camera link. Instructions on how to book your oral presentation will be given in the Week 1 workshop.

During the oral, you will explain the key ideas in your plan and respond to targeted questions about your design decisions. These questions will focus on your ability to explain, justify, and critically reflect on your learning sequence. You should be prepared to discuss points including, but not limited to, the following:

  • the learning needs of your intended learner group
  • your pedagogical approach
  • your choice of online platform and digital tools
  • how the four lessons connect as a coherent learning sequence
  • how your design supports accessibility, engagement, and learner diversity
  • potential barriers and enablers for implementation and how you would address them
  • how your design is informed by course readings and relevant literature

Please note no PowerPoint slides are required. You can bring in visual materials to support your explanation if applicable.

These interactive oral sessions, whether completed in person or via video link, will be video recorded to ensure there is an accurate record of your performance. Recordings will be retained for 12 months on a secure UQ system in accordance with UQ assessment policy.

For students completing the oral session online, you will require a working webcam, headphones, and you will be required to show the room during the oral.

This assessment task is to be completed live, either in person or online via video camera link. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools during the interview will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies in preparation, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Task 3: Learning Designs for Technology Integration Portfolio and Essay
assessments.task_description:
In this assignment, you will develop a portfolio of two detailed learning designs for technology-enhanced activities in your own professional teaching context. This task builds on Task 2. You are required to select two lessons from the four-lesson sequence plan you created in Task 2 and develop these lessons in greater detail.

Your two detailed learning designs should demonstrate how technology can be used to support meaningful learning in your selected teaching context. You should clearly identify the intended learners, curriculum learning objectives, learning activities, resources required, technologies used, and strategies for supporting learner engagement and participation.

Final submission

You will submit a 2000 to 2500-word essay, excluding references and appendices. Your two detailed learning designs should be included in the appendices.

Your essay should provide a rationale for the designs. In particular, your essay should:

  • explain how your two detailed learning designs align with the relevant curriculum learning objectives
  • justify how learning will occur through the two learning designs
  • identify and evaluate the instructional design features of your technology-enhanced activities using relevant models and literature
  • justify your designs in relation to current learning theories and pedagogies
  • articulate your beliefs about technology, teaching, and learning, and explain how these beliefs underpin your designs
  • address potential barriers to the implementation of your designs
  • outline an evaluation strategy for your designs
  • demonstrate breadth of knowledge of literature relating to learning and instructional design theories

Your two detailed learning designs should be included as appendices and should provide enough practical detail for the reader to understand how the lessons would be implemented.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 4(03 Aug - 23 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Technology Integration: Barriers and Enablers

Explore the barriers and enablers in utilising technology in your practice. Activities, readings and resources on Blackboard site. Assessment: Complete concept map and essay


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Technology Integration: Barriers and Enablers Explore the barriers and enablers in utilising technology in your practice. Activities, readings and resources on Blackboard site. Assessment: Complete concept map and essay

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 5 To Week 8(24 Aug - 20 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 2: Pedagogies: Creating pathways to the future.

Explore how pedagogies, theories and technologies are interwoven in practice. Discuss some contextual contemporary issues with colleagues with a view to potential solutions. Discover, use and critically evaluate technologies that may be applicable to your own pedagogy and pedagogical context. Activities, readings and resources on Blackboard site.

Assessment: Complete online platform lesson sequence and synthesis Please note that your appendices (including screen shots of your learning platform) are not counted in your word count.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Module 3: Learning design for effective technology-enhanced learning

Explore your own teaching and disciplinary context for opportunities to create meaningful learning designs to implement in practice. Create a portfolio of designs and evaluation strategies that you can use. Peer review your colleagues' designs and get valuable feedback on your own. Presentations during Workshops. Discover ways that you can meaningfully integrate technology into your own teaching practice. Activities, readings and resources on Blackboard site.

Assessment: Complete essay and learning design portfolio.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
